prompt.content:
│ │ # Storybook Setup │ ## Project Info │ | Property | Value | │ |----------|-------| │ | Version | 10.4.0-alpha.10 | │ | Renderer | @storybook/react | │ | Framework | @storybook/react-vite | │ | Builder | @storybook/builder-vite | │ | Config Dir | `.storybook` | │ | CSF Format | CSF Factory | │ | Addons | @storybook/addon-onboarding, @storybook/addon-themes, │ @storybook/addon-docs, @storybook/addon-designs, │ @storybook/addon-vitest, @storybook/addon-a11y, │ storybook-addon-pseudo-states, @chromatic-com/storybook | │ Attention: The following instructions must be followed in order to │ successfully set up Storybook in this project. Do not skip steps or │ attempt to do them out of order. │ Your goal is to make Storybook fully functional in this project by │ analyzing the codebase, │ configuring the preview with the right decorators, and writing stories │ for some components. │ The end state should be a Storybook where any component — from a small │ button to a full page — can be added without story-specific │ workarounds. All necessary providers, CSS, browser state, and network │ mocks should live in the shared preview so that just rendering the │ component in the story is enough. │ After each created story, run Vitest to verify it renders. │ If the test fails, read the error, fix the issue, and re-run until it │ passes before moving on. │ - Copy real patterns from the codebase │ - Keep the app code unchanged │ - Put the default setup in `.storybook/preview.tsx` │ - Keep app mocking and runtime setup in `.storybook/preview.tsx`, not │ in the stories │ ### Storybook Documentation Reference │ Use the following references to look up Storybook APIs, concepts, or │ examples: │ - Full docs index: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/llms.txthttps://storybook.js.org/llms.txt]8;; │ - See code snippets only with codeOnly=true param e.g. │ ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories.md?renderer=react&language=ts&codeOnly=truehttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories.md?renderer=react&language=ts&codeOnly=true]8;; │ Key documentation pages for this task: │ - Writing stories: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories.md?renderer=react&language=tshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories...]8;; │ - Decorators: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/decorators.md?renderer=react&language=tshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/deco...]8;; │ - Args: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/args.md?renderer=react&language=tshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/args.md?re...]8;; │ - Play functions: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/play-function.md?renderer=react&language=tshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/...]8;; │ - Vitest integration: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-tests/vitest-plugin.md?renderer=react&language=tshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-test...]8;; │ Fetch these URLs directly when you need guidance on Storybook APIs or │ patterns. │ ### Step 1: Analyze the codebase │ Read enough of the app to understand the full runtime environment │ before writing any stories. │ Do not stop at `main.tsx` or `App.tsx`. │ Follow imports into providers, pages, hooks, and shared components │ until you know: │ - which providers exist │ - which CSS files are injected │ - which queries fetch data │ - which browser-state reads happen │ - which portals and portal roots exist │ - which pages and components show the real usage patterns │ Example of what to copy: │ ```tsx │ // src/main.tsx │ import "./index.css"; │ import App from "./App"; │ import { SessionProvider } from "./contexts/SessionContext"; │ createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render( │ │ │ , │ ); │ ``` │ That means Storybook should copy: │ - the `index.css` import │ - the `SessionProvider` │ - the same provider order │ Example of tracing the app deeper: │ ```tsx │ // src/App.tsx │ function App() { │ const { products, loadMoreProducts } = useProducts(); │ const { currentUser, signOut } = useSession(); │ // ... │ } │ ``` │ ```ts │ // src/hooks/useProducts.ts │ const response = await fetch(apiBaseUrl + "/products?page=1"); │ ``` │ ```ts │ // src/hooks/useTheme.ts │ const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem("theme"); │ ``` │ That means the default Storybook setup should discover and prepare: │ - provider state │ - MSW handlers for queries │ - browser-state values that are actually read during render │ ### Step 2: Build one default app environment in preview │ Set up Storybook once so most stories work without story-specific │ setup. │ Start with the smallest faithful environment: │ - the real provider tree │ - the real root CSS │ - seeded browser state if the app reads it during render │ - MSW for network/data queries │ It is fine to seed browser state such as `localStorage`, │ `sessionStorage`, and cookies when the app reads them during render. │ Seed only the specific app-owned keys and values you need. │ Do not clear all `localStorage`, `sessionStorage`, or cookies, and do │ not reset Storybook's own state. │ Do not mock or redefine the browser runtime itself. │ The stories run in Vitest browser mode, so the real browser │ environment should already exist. │ ```tsx │ // .storybook/preview.tsx │ import '../src/index.css'; // import global styles │ import MockDate from 'mockdate'; │ import { definePreview } from 'storybook/preview'; │ import { SessionProvider } from '../src/contexts/SessionContext'; │ export default definePreview({ │ decorators: [ │ (Story) => ( │ │ │ │ ), │ ], │ async beforeEach() { │ localStorage.setItem('theme', 'dark'); │ localStorage.setItem('sidebar:open', 'true'); │ MockDate.set('2024-04-01T12:00:00Z'); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ Use this same idea for: │ - providers │ - root CSS │ - browser state │ - dates, and if the app logic depends on them during render then │ always use `mockdate` │ Example with the `mockdate` package: │ ```tsx │ import MockDate from 'mockdate'; │ import { definePreview } from 'storybook/preview'; │ export default definePreview({ │ async beforeEach() { │ MockDate.set('2024-04-01T12:00:00Z'); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ ### Step 3: Support portals with preview-body.html │ If the app uses portals, copy that setup into Storybook too. │ Look for patterns like: │ - `createPortal(...)` │ - modal, dialog, drawer, popover, tooltip, toast, or dropdown portal │ components │ - hard-coded roots such as `#portal-root`, `#modal-root`, │ `#drawer-root`, or `#toast-root` │ Example of what to copy: │ ```tsx │ // real component │ return createPortal(, │ document.getElementById("portal-root")!); │ ``` │ That means Storybook should create the same portal root in │ `.storybook/preview-body.html`: │ ```html │ │
│ ``` │ If the app uses multiple portal roots, create all of them there: │ ```html │ │
│
│
│ ``` │ If a library portals directly to `document.body`, do not add extra │ roots for it. │ Make sure the copied page shell, CSS, and layout still allow overlays, │ fixed positioning, and z-index stacking to render correctly. │ ### Step 4: Mock side effects globally │ All network/data queries should be handled by the default Storybook │ environment. │ - Always use `msw-storybook-addon` for query mocking. │ - If you introduce MSW, run `npx msw init ./public --save` to create │ the worker file. │ - Make sure Storybook serves `./public` as a static dir so │ `mockServiceWorker.js` is available. │ - Do not mock `fetch` directly. │ - Network/data queries should return deterministic mock data. │ - If you need to change dependencies, first check the lockfile and use │ that package manager for the change. │ Example of copying a real fetch pattern into shared handlers: │ ```ts │ // real app hook │ const response = await fetch( │ apiBaseUrl + │ "/products?" + │ new URLSearchParams({ │ page: "1", │ sort: "featured", │ }), │ ); │ ``` │ ```ts │ // .storybook/msw-handlers.ts │ import { http, HttpResponse } from "msw"; │ export const mswHandlers = { │ products: [ │ http.get("]8;;https://api.example.com/products",https://api.example.com/products",]8;; │ () => │ HttpResponse.json({ │ items: [ │ { │ id: "product-1", │ name: "Example product", │ description: "Mock product description", │ imageUrl: │ "]8;;https://images.example.com/product.jpg",https://images.example.com/product.jpg",]8;; │ price: 42, │ }, │ ], │ }), │ ), │ ], │ }; │ ``` │ ```tsx │ // .storybook/preview.tsx │ import { definePreview } from 'storybook/preview'; │ import { initialize, mswLoader } from 'msw-storybook-addon'; │ import { mswHandlers } from './msw-handlers'; │ initialize({ │ onUnhandledRequest: 'bypass', │ }); │ export default definePreview({ │ loaders: [mswLoader], │ parameters: { │ msw: { │ handlers: mswHandlers, │ }, │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ ```ts │ // .storybook/main.ts │ import type { StorybookConfig } from "@storybook/react-vite"; │ const config: StorybookConfig = { │ staticDirs: ["../public"], │ }; │ export default config; │ ``` │ Keep these mocks global. │ Do not put fetch mocks in individual stories. │ Only add handlers for requests that the shared preview setup or the │ stories actually use. │ Do not add catch-all handlers that can hide unrelated failures. │ If the defaults are not enough, improve the shared default setup │ instead. │ Seed browser state when needed, but do not mock `window`, `document`, │ `navigator`, observers, or similar runtime APIs. │ The only exception is `mockdate` when date-based rendering exists. │ ### Step 5: Write stories │ Try to find around 10 good candidate components for story files. │ Write colocated stories for top-level components, from low-level │ reusable components up to page components. │ Write up to 10 story files, or fewer only if the codebase clearly has │ fewer meaningful targets. │ The stories should use JSX copied from real usage patterns in: │ - pages │ - app shells │ - routes │ - tests │ - existing feature code │ As a rule of thumb, each story file should have around 3 story exports │ when the component or page has enough meaningful states. │ It can have more when the real usage supports it, up to 10 story │ exports in one file. │ Always show all imports explicitly in story and preview files. │ Do not rely on omitted or implied imports in examples or generated │ code. │ #### Story tags │ Every story meta must include the `ai-generated` tag to identify │ AI-created stories: │ ```tsx │ import preview from '#.storybook/preview'; │ import { expect } from 'storybook/test'; │ import { SomeComponent } from './SomeComponent'; │ const meta = preview.meta({ │ component: SomeComponent, │ tags: ['ai-generated'], │ }); │ export const Default = meta.story({ │ render: () => , │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ await expect(canvas.getByRole('button')).toBeVisible(); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ If a story could not be fully fixed after the self-healing loop (the │ test still fails │ or the rendering is incomplete), add the `needs-work` tag alongside │ `ai-generated`: │ ```ts │ const meta = preview.meta({ │ component: SomeComponent, │ tags: ['ai-generated', 'needs-work'], │ }); │ ``` │ #### Args vs render │ For simple components where props drive the state, prefer `args` │ stories — no `render` function needed: │ ```tsx │ import preview from '#.storybook/preview'; │ import { expect } from 'storybook/test'; │ import { Button } from './Button'; │ const meta = preview.meta({ │ component: Button, │ tags: ['ai-generated'], │ }); │ export const Primary = meta.story({ │ args: { │ variant: 'primary', │ children: 'Save', │ }, │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ await expect(canvas.getByRole('button', { name: /save/i │ })).toBeVisible(); │ }, │ }); │ export const Disabled = meta.story({ │ args: { │ variant: 'primary', │ disabled: true, │ children: 'Save', │ }, │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ await expect(canvas.getByRole('button')).toBeDisabled(); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ Use `render` when the story needs composition — wrapping the component │ in layout, combining multiple components, or passing children as JSX: │ ```tsx │ import preview from '#.storybook/preview'; │ import { expect } from 'storybook/test'; │ import { Button } from './Button'; │ import { Card } from './Card'; │ const meta = preview.meta({ │ component: Button, │ tags: ['ai-generated'], │ }); │ export const InsideCard = meta.story({ │ render: () => ( │ │ │ │ ), │ play: async ({ canvas, userEvent }) => { │ await expect(canvas.getByRole('button', { name: /save/i │ })).toBeVisible(); │ await userEvent.click(canvas.getByRole('button', { name: /save/i })); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ Keep app mocking and runtime setup in preview, not in the stories. │ Do not build large story-specific harnesses. │ Do not write story files for subcomponents, hooks, contexts, or │ helpers. │ Do not create new application components. │ Do not add a custom `title`. │ Do not stop after only a few easy targets if the codebase has more │ meaningful components or pages available. │ ### Step 6: Write a play function for every story │ Every named story export must have a `play` function. │ The `play` function is not optional, even for simple stories. │ The purpose of the `play` function is to prove that the story actually │ works in the copied Storybook environment: │ - the story renders something real and non-empty │ - the decorators provide the needed context │ - the CSS is applied well enough for the intended state to be visible │ - the MSW mocks or seeded browser state are actually being used │ - important interactions, async loading states, and portals behave │ correctly │ Use `play` functions to verify behavior, not just to click around. │ A story without assertions is incomplete. │ Use tools from `storybook/test` such as: │ - `expect` │ - `waitFor` │ Prefer `canvas` and `userEvent` from the `play` context. │ Do not destructure `canvasElement` just to create `const canvas = │ within(canvasElement)`. │ Do not import `userEvent` from `storybook/test`; use `userEvent` from │ the `play` context instead. │ Only use `canvasElement.ownerDocument` when you need to query outside │ the canvas, such as for portals. │ Example: │ ```tsx │ import type { StoryObj } from "@storybook/react-vite"; │ export const FilledForm: Story = { │ play: async ({ canvas, userEvent }) => { │ const emailInput = canvas.getByLabelText("email", { │ selector: "input", │ }); │ await userEvent.type(emailInput, "example-email@email.com", { │ delay: 100, │ }); │ const passwordInput = canvas.getByLabelText("password", { │ selector: "input", │ }); │ await userEvent.type(passwordInput, "ExamplePassword", { │ delay: 100, │ }); │ const submitButton = canvas.getByRole("button"); │ await userEvent.click(submitButton); │ }, │ }; │ ``` │ The assertions should match the real pattern you copied: │ - for provider-backed stories, assert the provider-dependent UI │ appears correctly │ - for mocked-data stories, wait for the mocked data to appear and │ assert on it │ - for CSS-sensitive states, assert on visibility, text layout, │ class-driven states, or meaningful computed styles │ - for routing or navigation stories, assert the routed state or │ navigation outcome │ - for portal stories, query from `canvasElement.ownerDocument` when │ the UI renders outside the canvas │ Examples of useful checks: │ - a themed button has the expected label and is visibly enabled or │ disabled │ - a modal opened through a decorator or provider is visible in the │ portal root │ - mocked API data appears in the page instead of a loading spinner │ forever │ - a selected tab actually shows the selected panel │ - a toast, alert, or badge has the expected accessible text and visual │ state │ - a CSS class or computed style confirms the real state that matters │ ### Step 7: Prove CSS is loaded in exactly one story named `CssCheck` │ In exactly one story, named `CssCheck`, assert a component-specific │ computed style. `toBeVisible` passes on an unstyled component; a │ concrete style value proves the shared preview loaded the app's CSS. │ Pick a visually distinctive component, read a styling value from its │ source, and assert it with `getComputedStyle`: │ ```tsx │ export const CssCheck: Story = { │ args: { children: "Submit" }, │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ const button = canvas.getByRole("button", { name: /submit/i }); │ // PrimaryButton uses bg-blue-600 — fails if Tailwind / global CSS did │ not load. │ await expect(getComputedStyle(button).backgroundColor).toBe("rgb(37, │ 99, 235)"); │ }, │ }; │ ``` │ ### Step 8: Cover the patterns you found │ Write stories for the real patterns in the codebase, for example: │ - a low-level reusable component in real JSX usage │ - a provider-backed component │ - a browser-state-backed component │ - a fetched-data component │ - a real page component │ Use `App.tsx` to inspect the real provider tree and usage patterns, │ but do not make a story for `App` when the codebase has actual page │ components. │ Example page story: │ ```tsx │ import preview from '#.storybook/preview'; │ import { expect } from 'storybook/test'; │ import { ProductPage } from './ProductPage'; │ const meta = preview.meta({ │ component: ProductPage, │ tags: ['ai-generated'], │ }); │ export const Default = meta.story({ │ render: () => , │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ await expect( │ canvas.getByRole('heading', { name: /products/i }), │ ).toBeVisible(); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ ### Step 9: Verify both rendering and types │ As you work, verify the stories with Vitest: │ ```bash │ npx vitest --project storybook │ ``` │ Also verify types so you catch missing required props, broken imports, │ and preview typing issues. Run the same TypeScript command the project │ itself uses. │ ```bash │ │ ``` │ After verification passes, review every changed file and remove │ anything that is not needed for the final solution, especially debug │ fixes, overly broad mocks, unnecessary dependencies, and eval │ artifacts. │ Keep iterating until: │ - every story you wrote passes │ - every story you wrote has a meaningful passing `play` function │ - the changed stories and preview setup pass the project's real │ TypeScript check │ - the rendered output looks sensible │ - the default global mocked environment is strong enough that stories │ do not need manual fetch overrides │ - stories no longer fail because the shared preview setup and story │ JSX are fixed │ - all passing stories have `tags: ['ai-generated']` in their meta │ - any stories that still need work have `tags: ['ai-generated', │ 'needs-work']` in their meta

content.thinking: Let me start by analyzing the codebase to understand the full runtime environment before writing any stories.

input.prompt:
Explore this codebase thoroughly. I need to understand:

1. The entry point (main.tsx/index.tsx) - what providers, CSS imports, and setup exists
2. App.tsx - the provider tree, routing, layout
3. All context providers used
4. All hooks that fetch data (look for fetch, axios, useQuery, etc.)
5. Browser state reads (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies)
6. Portal usage (createPortal, modal roots, etc.)
7. Pages and route structure
8. Key reusable components
9. CSS setup (Tailwind, CSS modules, styled-components, etc.)
10. Package manager used (look for lock files)
11. TypeScript config and type-check command
12. Any existing MSW setup or test utilities

Be very thorough - read into providers, hooks, pages, and shared components. I need the full picture of the runtime environment.

     4→import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'
     5→
     6→import { store } from './app-state'

    17→      <StoreProvider store={store}>
    18→        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
    19→          <GlobalStyle />
    20→          <AppRoutes />
    21→        </ThemeProvider>
    22→      </StoreProvider>
    23→    </Router>
    24→  )

1→import { useLayoutEffect } from 'react'

     3→
     4→import { CategoryListPage } from './pages/CategoryListPage'
     5→import { CategoryDetailPage } from './pages/CategoryDetailPage'
     6→import { RestaurantDetailPage } from './pages/RestaurantDetailPage'
     7→import { HomePage } from './pages/HomePage'
     8→import { CheckoutPage } from './pages/CheckoutPage'
     9→import { SuccessPage } from './pages/SuccessPage'
    10→
    11→export const AppRoutes: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<unknown>> = ({ children }) => {
    12→  const location = useLocation()
    13→  // Scroll to top when a path changes
    14→  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    15→    window.scrollTo(0, 0)
    16→  }, [location.pathname])
    17→
    18→  return (
    19→    <>
    20→      <div id="modal" />
    21→      <Routes>
    22→        <Route path="/categories" element={<CategoryListPage />} />
    23→        <Route path="/categories/:id" element={<CategoryDetailPage />} />
    24→        <Route path="/restaurants/:id" element={<RestaurantDetailPage />} />
    25→        <Route path="/checkout" element={<CheckoutPage />} />
    26→        <Route path="/success" element={<SuccessPage />} />
    27→        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
    28→        {children}
    29→      </Routes>
    30→    </>
    31→  )
    32→}

1→import { createSlice, CaseReducer, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→import { CartItem } from '../cart'
     4→
     5→export type OrderState = {
     6→  items: CartItem[]
     7→}
     8→
     9→const saveOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState, PayloadAction<CartItem[]>> = (state, action) => {
    10→  state.items = action.payload
    11→}
    12→
    13→const clearOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState> = (state) => {
    14→  state.items = []
    15→}
    16→
    17→const initialState: OrderState = {
    18→  items: [],
    19→}
    20→
    21→const orderSlice = createSlice({
    22→  name: 'order',
    23→  initialState,
    24→  reducers: {
    25→    saveOrderAction: saveOrder,
    26→    clearOrderAction: clearOrder,
    27→  },
    28→})
    29→
    30→// Extract the action creators object and the reducer
    31→export const { actions, reducer } = orderSlice
    32→
    33→// Extract and export each action creator by name
    34→export const { saveOrderAction, clearOrderAction } = actions
    35→
    36→// Export the reducer, either as a default or named export
    37→export default reducer
    38→

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→export const useKey = (key: string, keyDownCb = () => {}, keyUpCb = () => {}): boolean => {
     4→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false)
     5→  const match = useCallback((event: any) => key.toLowerCase() === event.key.toLowerCase(), [key])
     6→
     7→  const onKeyDown = useCallback(
     8→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
     9→      if (match(event)) {
    10→        setPressed(true)
    11→        keyDownCb()
    12→      }
    13→    },
    14→    [keyDownCb, match]
    15→  )
    16→
    17→  const onKeyUp = useCallback(
    18→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
    19→      if (match(event)) {
    20→        setPressed(false)
    21→        keyUpCb()
    22→      }
    23→    },
    24→    [keyUpCb, match]
    25→  )
    26→
    27→  useEffect(() => {
    28→    globalThis.addEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    29→    globalThis.addEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    30→
    31→    return () => {
    32→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    33→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    34→    }
    35→  }, [key, onKeyDown, onKeyUp])
    36→
    37→  return pressed
    38→}
    39→

Now I have a comprehensive picture of the codebase. Let me create a thorough summary: ## Codebase Exploration Summary ### 1. **Entry Point (main.tsx/index.tsx)** **Location:** `/Users/yannbraga/open-source/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-28T22-04-26Z-29004daa/project/src/index.tsx` The app is bootstrapped with: - React 19 with `StrictMode` wrapper - `createRoot` from `react-dom/client` - App component rendering directly - Service worker disabled (calling `unregister()`) - Single root DOM mount point at `#root` ```tsx // index.tsx structure import App from './App' import * as serviceWorker from './serviceWorker' const root = createRoot(document.querySelector('#root')) root.render() serviceWorker.unregister() ``` ### 2. **App.tsx - Provider Tree & Setup** **Location:** `/Users/yannbraga/open-source/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-28T22-04-26Z-29004daa/project/src/App.tsx` Provider hierarchy (nested order): ``` Router (react-router-dom) └─ StoreProvider (Redux) └─ ThemeProvider (styled-components) └─ GlobalStyle └─ AppRoutes ``` Theme handling: - **Dark mode support:** `use-dark-mode` hook manages light/dark theme state - **Theme objects:** `lightTheme` and `darkTheme` from `src/styles/theme.ts` - **Global styles:** Applied via `styled-components` createGlobalStyle ### 3. **Context Providers Used** 1. **React Router DOM** - `BrowserRouter` for client-side routing 2. **Redux** - `react-redux` `Provider` wrapping the Redux store 3. **styled-components** - `ThemeProvider` passing theme object down 4. **Dark Mode** - `use-dark-mode` library (not a context provider, but manages state globally) No custom React Context providers found in the codebase. ### 4. **Data Fetching Hooks** **Location:** `/Users/yannbraga/open-source/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-28T22-04-26Z-29004daa/project/src/api/hooks.ts` Three custom hooks for data fetching: - `useFetchRestaurants()` - fetches all restaurants - `useFetchRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)` - fetches filtered restaurants - `useFetchRestaurant(id)` - fetches single restaurant by ID Pattern used: - Manual state management (useState for status, data, retry) - axios for HTTP requests - Error handling via error.response.status - No useQuery/React Query - all manual promise chains - API calls made on dependency changes via useEffect **API Implementation:** `/Users/yannbraga/open-source/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-28T22-04-26Z-29004daa/project/src/api/index.ts` - Uses axios with a simple caching mechanism (Map-based) - Base URL: `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants` - Conditional API selection based on environment (conditional-logic.ts exists as alternative) - file.content:
import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

file.content:
import { DefaultTheme } from 'styled-components'

declare module 'styled-components' {
  export interface DefaultTheme {
    name: 'light' | 'dark'
    fonts: {
      family: string
    }
    spacing: typeof spacing
    color: typeof color
    borderRadius: typeof borderRadius
    boxShadow: typeof boxShadow
    typography: typeof typography
  }
}

const baseColors = {
  white: '#FFFFFF ',
  black: '#202020',
  red: {
    base: '#D70808',
    light1: '#E57373',
    light2: '#EF9A9A',
    dark1: '#C62828',
    dark2: '#B71C1C',
  },
  otherBlack: '#2C2C2C ',
  grey: {
    base: '#909090',
    light1: '#A6A6A6',
    light2: '#BCBCBC',
    light3: '#D2D2D2',
    light4: '#E9E9E9',
    light5: '#F5F6F7',
    light6: '#F9F9F9',
    dark1: '#797979',
    dark2: '#636363',
    dark3: '#4D4D4D',
    dark4: '#363636',
    dark5: '#2C2C2C',
    dark6: '#202020',
  },
  green: {
    base: '#E5F8BC',
    light1: '#E8F9C3',
    light2: '#EAF9C9',
    light3: '#EDFAD0',
    light4: '#EFFBD7',
    light5: '#F2FCDE',
    dark1: '#CEDFA9',
    dark2: '#B7C696',
    dark3: '#A0AE84',
    dark4: '#899571',
    dark5: '#737C5E',
  },
  blue: {
    base: '#B1DDE4',
    light1: '#B9E0E7',
    light2: '#C1E4E9',
    light3: '#C8E7EC',
    light4: '#D0EBEF',
    light5: '#D8EEF2',
    dark1: '#9FC7CD',
    dark2: '#8EB1B6',
    dark3: '#7C9BA0',
    dark4: '#6A8589',
    dark5: '#596f72',
  },
}

const spaceUnit = 1

const borderRadius = {
  xs: '4px',
  s: '8px',
  m: '16px',
  l: '24px',
  xl: '32px',
  xxl: '40px',
  round: '50%',
}

const spacing = {
  xxs: `${0.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
  xs: `${0.5 * spaceUnit}em`,
  s: `${spaceUnit}em`,
  m: `${1.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
  l: `${2 * spaceUnit}em`,
  xl: `${3.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
  xxl: `${5.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
}

const boxShadow = {
  card: '0px 14px 26px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08)',
  inner: 'inset 0 3px 0 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',
  outerBorder: `0 0 0 1px ${baseColors.blue.dark2}, 0 0 0 4px ${baseColors.blue.light5}`,
}

const typography = {
  fontSize: {
    body: '1.125rem',
    bodyS: '1rem',
    bodyXS: '0.9rem',
    bodyXXS: '0.72rem',
    heading1: '2.74rem',
    heading2: '2.19rem',
    heading3: '1.75rem',
    heading4: '1.4rem',
  },
  fontWeight: {
    black: '900',
    bold: '700',
    medium: '500',
    regular: '400',
  },
}

const color = {
  accentText: baseColors.black,
  badgeBackground: baseColors.grey.light4,
  badgeText: baseColors.grey.dark2,
  cartButtonText: baseColors.grey.base,
  bannerBackground: baseColors.blue.base,
  buttonClear: 'transparent',
  buttonClearHover: baseColors.grey.light5,
  buttonPrimary: baseColors.grey.dark6,
  buttonPrimaryHover: baseColors.grey.dark5, // recheck
  buttonSecondary: baseColors.green.base,
  buttonSecondaryHover: baseColors.green.dark1, // recheck
  buttonText: baseColors.white,
  cardBackground: baseColors.grey.light6,
  checkoutTopBackground: baseColors.green.base,
  checkoutBottomBackground: baseColors.grey.light6,
  error: baseColors.red.dark2,
  formBackground: baseColors.white,
  foodItemBackground: baseColors.white,
  footerBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
  headerBackground: baseColors.white,
  restaurantDetailBackground: baseColors.white,
  headerBorder: baseColors.grey.light4,
  inputBackground: baseColors.grey.light5,
  inputHint: baseColors.grey.dark1,
  inputIcon: baseColors.grey.dark6,
  label: baseColors.grey.dark4,
  labelActive: baseColors.grey.dark6,
  menuSectionBackground: baseColors.grey.light6,
  stepsIndicatorInnerBar: baseColors.grey.dark6,
  stepsIndicatorOuterBar: baseColors.grey.light5,
  orderSummaryBackground: baseColors.white,
  overlayBackground: baseColors.white,
  overlayHeader: baseColors.grey.light5,
  primaryText: baseColors.otherBlack,
  secondaryText: baseColors.grey.dark2,
  // This is just used to demo a11y violations, use it in the Review component
  bannerText: baseColors.blue.dark3,
  reviewText: baseColors.blue.dark5,
  screenBackground: baseColors.white,
  sidebarHeader: baseColors.grey.dark4,
  sidebarFooter: baseColors.white,
  skeletonBase: baseColors.grey.light4,
  skeletonHighlight: baseColors.grey.light5,
  topBannerBackground: baseColors.green.base,
  newRestaurantTag: baseColors.green.base,
  white: baseColors.white,
  black: baseColors.black,
}

export const lightTheme: DefaultTheme = {
  borderRadius,
  boxShadow,
  color,
  fonts: {
    family: 'NunitoSans, sans-serif',
  },
  name: 'light',
  spacing,
  typography,
}

export const darkTheme: DefaultTheme = {
  ...lightTheme,
  boxShadow: {
    ...boxShadow,
    outerBorder: `0 0 0 2px ${baseColors.blue.dark5}, 0 0 0 4px ${baseColors.blue.base}`,
  },
  color: {
    ...lightTheme.color,
    badgeBackground: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    badgeText: baseColors.grey.light1,
    cartButtonText: baseColors.grey.dark2,
    bannerBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    buttonClearHover: baseColors.grey.dark4, // recheck
    buttonPrimary: baseColors.green.base,
    buttonPrimaryHover: baseColors.green.dark1, // recheck
    buttonSecondary: baseColors.green.base,
    buttonText: baseColors.otherBlack,
    cardBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    checkoutTopBackground: baseColors.grey.dark6,
    checkoutBottomBackground: baseColors.grey.dark1,
    formBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    foodItemBackground: baseColors.otherBlack,
    headerBackground: baseColors.grey.dark6,
    restaurantDetailBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    headerBorder: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    inputBackground: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    inputIcon: baseColors.white,
    inputHint: baseColors.grey.light1,
    label: baseColors.grey.light4,
    labelActive: baseColors.white,
    menuSectionBackground: baseColors.grey.dark6,
    stepsIndicatorInnerBar: baseColors.green.base,
    stepsIndicatorOuterBar: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    orderSummaryBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    overlayBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    overlayHeader: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    primaryText: baseColors.white,
    reviewText: baseColors.blue.base,
    screenBackground: baseColors.grey.dark6,
    sidebarHeader: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    sidebarFooter: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    skeletonBase: baseColors.grey.dark3,
    skeletonHighlight: baseColors.grey.dark2,
    topBannerBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
  },
  name: 'dark',
}

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}
   101→

1→import { useLayoutEffect } from 'react'
     2→import { Routes, Route, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'
     3→
     4→import { CategoryListPage } from './pages/CategoryListPage'
     5→import { CategoryDetailPage } from './pages/CategoryDetailPage'
     6→import { RestaurantDetailPage } from './pages/RestaurantDetailPage'
     7→import { HomePage } from './pages/HomePage'
     8→import { CheckoutPage } from './pages/CheckoutPage'
     9→import { SuccessPage } from './pages/SuccessPage'
    10→
    11→export const AppRoutes: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<unknown>> = ({ children }) => {
    12→  const location = useLocation()
    13→  // Scroll to top when a path changes
    14→  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    15→    window.scrollTo(0, 0)
    16→  }, [location.pathname])
    17→
    18→  return (
    19→    <>
    20→      <div id="modal" />
    21→      <Routes>
    22→        <Route path="/categories" element={<CategoryListPage />} />
    23→        <Route path="/categories/:id" element={<CategoryDetailPage />} />
    24→        <Route path="/restaurants/:id" element={<RestaurantDetailPage />} />
    25→        <Route path="/checkout" element={<CheckoutPage />} />
    26→        <Route path="/success" element={<SuccessPage />} />
    27→        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
    28→        {children}
    29→      </Routes>
    30→    </>
    31→  )
    32→}
    33→

     2→export * from './hooks'

content.thinking:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me plan and execute the work.

Key findings:
1. **Entry point**: `src/index.tsx` - StrictMode wrapping App
2. **App.tsx**: Router > StoreProvider (Redux) > ThemeProvider (styled-components) > GlobalStyle + AppRoutes
3. **Providers**: BrowserRouter, Redux Provider, styled-components ThemeProvider
4. **Dark mode**: `use-dark-mode` hook
5. **Data fetching**: axios to `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants` with three hooks


6. **Portal**: `#modal` div with Portal component using `createPortal`
7. **Styling**: styled-components with GlobalStyle, plus carousel and skeleton CSS imports
8. **State management**: Redux with cart and order slices
9. **Package manager**: yarn, with type checking via `yarn check` or `tsc --noEmit`
10. **MSW**: installed with service worker in public folder, but handlers aren't configured yet

Now I need to examine the key components and pages to figure out what stories to write.

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report of all the component interfaces and usage patterns.

## Complete Component Interfaces and Usage Patterns Report

---

### 1. **Button Component**
**File:** `/src/components/Button/Button.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type ButtonProperties = {
  clear?: boolean           // Clear button styles (text only)
  round?: boolean           // Round border radius
  large?: boolean           // Large size variant
  icon?: IconName           // Icon name to display
  iconSize?: number         // Icon size
  disabled?: boolean        // Disabled state
  children?: string | React.ReactNode
  onClick?: () => void
} & Omit<React.ComponentProps<'button'>, keyof DefaultProperties>
```

**Key Features:**
- Renders as `<button type="button">`
- Styled with styled-components
- Supports icon display via Icon component
- Theme-aware colors (primary, clear variants)
- Responsive padding adjustments
- Focus state with box-shadow
- Hover and disabled states

**Dependencies:**
- `styled-components` for styling
- `Icon` component for icon display
- `useTheme` hook from styled-components
- Theme object with `color`, `boxShadow`, `borderRadius`

---

### 2. **RestaurantCard Component**
**File:** `/src/components/RestaurantCard/RestaurantCard.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type RestaurantCardProps = {
  name: string
  rating?: number
  specialty: string
  photoUrl: string
  isClosed?: boolean
  categories?: string[]
  isLoading?: boolean
  isNew?: boolean
  onClick?: () => void
  className?: string
}
```

**Children Components Used:**
- `RestaurantCardSkeleton` (internal)
- `Badge` component (for categories)
- `Review` component (for rating display)
- `Heading` component (for name)
- `Body` component (for description)

**Key Features:**
- Loading skeleton support via `react-loading-skeleton`
- "New" tag display
- Closed restaurant overlay (grayscale image + text)
- Lazy-loaded images
- Responsive max-width
- Category badges

**Dependencies:**
- `react-loading-skeleton`
- `styled-components`
- `Badge`, `Review`, `Body`, `Heading` components
- Theme object with colors, borderRadius

---

### 3. **Header Component**
**File:** `/src/components/Header/Header.tsx`

**Props Interface (Component):**
```typescript
type HeaderComponentProps = {
  isCartVisible?: boolean
  cartItems?: CartItem[]
  totalPrice?: number
  logoOnly?: boolean
  sticky?: boolean
  toggleCartVisibility?: () => void
  goToCheckout?: () => void
  saveItem?: (item: CartItem) => void
}
```

**Props Interface (Container - `Header`):**
```typescript
type HeaderProps = {
  sticky?: boolean
}
```

**Children Components Used:**
- `Logo`
- `Button` (for navigation and cart toggle)
- `ShoppingCartMenu`
- `ThemeToggle` (internal, uses Button)

**Key Features:**
- Two-part export: presentational `HeaderComponent` and connected `Header` container
- Uses Redux selectors: `selectCartVisibility`, `selectCartItems`, `selectCartTotal`
- Uses Redux actions: `toggleVisibilityAction`, `saveItemAction`
- Sticky/relative positioning toggle
- Dark mode toggle via `use-dark-mode` hook
- Cart button with total price display
- Navigation links to Home and Categories (mobile-hidden)

**Dependencies:**
- `react-router-dom` (Link, useNavigate)
- `use-dark-mode` package
- Redux hooks and cart state
- `styled-components`
- `ShoppingCartMenu`, `Button`, `Logo` components
- Theme object with colors

---

### 4. **Footer Component**
**File:** `/src/components/Footer/Footer.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - fully self-contained with hardcoded links

**Children Components Used:**
- `Logo` (with `logoOnly` prop)
- `FooterCard` (for link sections)

**Key Features:**
- 4-column grid layout (responsive: 1 → 3 → 4 columns)
- Navigation links (Home, Categories, About, Login)
- Social media links
- App store badges (images)
- White text on footer background

**Dependencies:**
- `styled-components`
- `Logo`, `FooterCard` components
- Static image imports (googleplay-banner, appstore-banner)
- Theme object with colors

---

### 5. **Badge Component**
**File:** `/src/components/Badge/Badge.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type BadgeProps = {
  text: string
  className?: string
}
```

**Children Components Used:**
- `Body` component (for text display)

**Key Features:**
- Small inline badge with background
- Text is capitalized and styled
- Minimal padding
- No interactive behavior

**Dependencies:**
- `styled-components`
- `Body` component
- Theme object with colors, borderRadius

---

### 6. **Modal Component**
**File:** `/src/components/Modal/Modal.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type ModalProps = {
  isOpen: boolean
  onClose: () => void
  children?: React.ReactNode
}
```

**Key Features:**
- Uses `react-transition-group` for animations
- Portal rendering to #modal element
- Escape key closes modal (via `useKey` hook)
- Body scroll lock when open (via `useLockBodyScroll` hook)
- Close button in top-right corner
- Backdrop click closes modal
- Different animations for mobile (slide-up) vs desktop (scale)

**Dependencies:**
- `react-transition-group` (CSSTransition)
- `Portal` component
- Custom hooks: `useKey`, `useLockBodyScroll`
- `Button` component (for close button)
- Styled components from Modal.styles.tsx

**Modal.styles.tsx Details:**
- `ModalContent`: Fixed positioning with animations
- `Backdrop`: Full-screen semi-transparent overlay
- `TopBar`: Close button container
- Different animations for mobile/desktop

---

### 7. **ShoppingCartMenu Component**
**File:** `/src/components/ShoppingCartMenu/ShoppingCartMenu.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type ShoppingCartMenuProps = {
  isOpen: boolean
  totalPrice: number
  onClose: () => void
  cartItems: CartItem[]
  onGoToCheckoutClick?: () => void
  onItemChange: (item: CartItem) => void
}
```

**Children Components Used:**
- `Sidebar` (container)
- Internal `ShoppingCartMenuItem` component
- Internal `Footer` component
- `Body` component (for text)
- `Select` component (for quantity)

**Key Features:**
- Wraps in `Sidebar` component
- Displays cart items with quantity selector (0-10)
- Shows total price
- Checkout button (disabled if total is 0)
- Item change handler for quantity updates

**Dependencies:**
- `styled-components`
- `Sidebar`, `Button`, `Select`, `Body` components
- `CartItem` type from app-state
- `toCurrency` helper

---

### 8. **TopBanner Component**
**File:** `/src/components/TopBanner/TopBanner.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type TopBannerProps = {
  title?: string
  photoUrl?: string
  onBackClick?: () => void
}
```

**Key Features:**
- Background image support or solid color fallback
- Optional title display (inverted color if photo present)
- Fixed height (240px)
- Centered content
- Responsive background sizing

**Dependencies:**
- `styled-components`
- `Heading` component
- Theme object with colors

---

### 9. **HomePage**
**File:** `/src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - no props

**Children Components Used:**
- `PageTemplate` (wrapper)
- `Banner`
- `RestaurantsSection` (title prop)
- `AwardWinningSection`
- `CategoriesSection` (categories prop)

**Key Features:**
- Landing page layout
- Multiple sections with spacing
- Imports category stub data
- Uses PageTemplate wrapper

---

### 10. **CategoryListPage**
**File:** `/src/pages/CategoryListPage/CategoryListPage.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - no props

**Children Components Used:**
- `PageTemplate`
- `TopBanner` (with title)
- `CategoryList` (with categories)
- `Heading`, `Body` components
- `useNavigate` hook

**Key Features:**
- Uses React Router for navigation
- Back button navigation via `navigate(-1)`
- Stub categories data
- Descriptive text section

---

### 11. **RestaurantDetailPage**
**File:** `/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - uses route params (`useParams<'id'>`)

**Children Components Used:**
- `PageTemplate` (type: "sticky-header")
- `FoodItemModal` (conditional)
- `TopBanner` (with photo)
- `ErrorBlock` (error states)
- `Spinner` (loading state)
- `Heading`, `Body` components
- `Badge`, `Review` components
- `AnimatedIllustration` (for errors)
- `FoodSection` (for menu items: food, dessert, drinks)

**Key Features:**
- Fetches restaurant data via `useFetchRestaurant(id)` hook
- Redux integration: cart items selection and actions
- Three error states: 500, 404, loading
- Modal for food item selection
- Multiple menu sections
- Back button navigation

**Dependencies:**
- `react-router-dom` (useParams, useNavigate)
- Redux hooks and cart actions
- API hook: `useFetchRestaurant`
- Custom components and state management

---

### 12. **CheckoutPage**
**File:** `/src/pages/CheckoutPage/CheckoutPage.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - no props

**Children Components Used:**
- `PageTemplate` (type: "basic")
- `MultiStepForm` (registration form)
- `OrderSummary` (cart items)
- `Heading` component

**Key Features:**
- Two-column layout (form + order summary)
- Uses Redux to get cart items
- Responsive layout (stacked on mobile, side-by-side on desktop)
- Form spans full width on mobile, fixed width on desktop

---

### 13. **SuccessPage**
**File:** `/src/pages/SuccessPage/SuccessPage.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - no props

**Children Components Used:**
- `PageTemplate` (type: "basic")
- `TopBanner` (with title)
- `OrderSummary` (with order items)
- `Heading`, `Body` components

**Key Features:**
- Displays order confirmation
- Shows estimated delivery time
- Uses Redux to get order items (`selectOrderItems`)
- Decorative illustration (ladies-sushi.svg)
- Uses order state (not cart state)

---

### 14. **Types**
**File:** `/src/types/index.ts`

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
type ShopItem = {
  id: number
  name: string
  price: number
  imageUrl?: string
  description?: string
}

type FoodMenuItem = {
  id: number
  name: string
  description?: string
  price: number
}

type Restaurant = {
  name: string
  id?: string
  mapsUrl?: string
  rating?: number
  url?: string
  address?: string
  specialty: string
  photoUrl: string
  isClosed?: boolean
  categories?: string[]
  isLoading?: boolean
  isNew?: boolean
  menu: {
    food: FoodMenuItem[]
    dessert: FoodMenuItem[]
    drinks: FoodMenuItem[]
  }
}
```

---

## Key State Management (Cart)

**CartItem Type** (extends ShopItem):
```typescript
interface CartItem extends ShopItem {
  quantity: number
}
```

**Cart State:**
```typescript
type CartState = {
  visible: boolean
  items: CartItem[]
}
```

**Selectors:**
- `selectCartVisibility`: boolean
- `selectCartItems`: CartItem[]
- `selectCartTotal`: number (sum of quantity * price)
- `selectCartItemsCount`: number (total quantity)

**Actions:**
- `toggleVisibilityAction`: toggle cart menu visibility
- `saveItemAction(item)`: add or update item quantity
- `clearItemAction(item)`: remove item entirely
- `removeItemAction(item)`: decrement quantity or remove
- `clearCartAction`: clear all items and hide cart

---

## Common Pattern Summary

1. **Presentational vs Container Pattern**: Header component uses both (HeaderComponent + Header)
2. **Redux Integration**: Header, RestaurantDetailPage, CheckoutPage, SuccessPage use Redux
3. **Styled-components**: All components use styled-components for styling
4. **Theme Integration**: All styled components access theme via `useTheme()` hook
5. **Hooks Usage**: Custom hooks for keyboard (useKey), body scroll lock (useLockBodyScroll)
6. **React Router**: Navigation via Link and useNavigate across pages
7. **Error Handling**: RestaurantDetailPage handles loading, errors, and success states
8. **Modal/Sidebar Pattern**: Uses Portal and CSSTransition for animations

All files are located under `/src/` with components in `/src/components/`, pages in `/src/pages/`, and types in `/src/types/`.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report of all the component interfaces and usage patterns.

## Complete Component Interfaces and Usage Patterns Report

---

### 1. **Button Component**
**File:** `/src/components/Button/Button.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type ButtonProperties = {
  clear?: boolean           // Clear button styles (text only)
  round?: boolean           // Round border radius
  large?: boolean           // Large size variant
  icon?: IconName           // Icon name to display
  iconSize?: number         // Icon size
  disabled?: boolean        // Disabled state
  children?: string | React.ReactNode
  onClick?: () => void
} & Omit<React.ComponentProps<'button'>, keyof DefaultProperties>
```

**Key Features:**
- Renders as `<button type="button">`
- Styled with styled-components
- Supports icon display via Icon component
- Theme-aware colors (primary, clear variants)
- Responsive padding adjustments
- Focus state with box-shadow
- Hover and disabled states

**Dependencies:**
- `styled-components` for styling
- `Icon` component for icon display
- `useTheme` hook from styled-components
- Theme object with `color`, `boxShadow`, `borderRadius`

---

### 2. **RestaurantCard Component**
**File:** `/src/components/RestaurantCard/RestaurantCard.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type RestaurantCardProps = {
  name: string
  rating?: number
  specialty: string
  photoUrl: string
  isClosed?: boolean
  categories?: string[]
  isLoading?: boolean
  isNew?: boolean
  onClick?: () => void
  className?: string
}
```

**Children Components Used:**
- `RestaurantCardSkeleton` (internal)
- `Badge` component (for categories)
- `Review` component (for rating display)
- `Heading` component (for name)
- `Body` component (for description)

**Key Features:**
- Loading skeleton support via `react-loading-skeleton`
- "New" tag display
- Closed restaurant overlay (grayscale image + text)
- Lazy-loaded images
- Responsive max-width
- Category badges

**Dependencies:**
- `react-loading-skeleton`
- `styled-components`
- `Badge`, `Review`, `Body`, `Heading` components
- Theme object with colors, borderRadius

---

### 3. **Header Component**
**File:** `/src/components/Header/Header.tsx`

**Props Interface (Component):**
```typescript
type HeaderComponentProps = {
  isCartVisible?: boolean
  cartItems?: CartItem[]
  totalPrice?: number
  logoOnly?: boolean
  sticky?: boolean
  toggleCartVisibility?: () => void
  goToCheckout?: () => void
  saveItem?: (item: CartItem) => void
}
```

**Props Interface (Container - `Header`):**
```typescript
type HeaderProps = {
  sticky?: boolean
}
```

**Children Components Used:**
- `Logo`
- `Button` (for navigation and cart toggle)
- `ShoppingCartMenu`
- `ThemeToggle` (internal, uses Button)

**Key Features:**
- Two-part export: presentational `HeaderComponent` and connected `Header` container
- Uses Redux selectors: `selectCartVisibility`, `selectCartItems`, `selectCartTotal`
- Uses Redux actions: `toggleVisibilityAction`, `saveItemAction`
- Sticky/relative positioning toggle
- Dark mode toggle via `use-dark-mode` hook
- Cart button with total price display
- Navigation links to Home and Categories (mobile-hidden)

**Dependencies:**
- `react-router-dom` (Link, useNavigate)
- `use-dark-mode` package
- Redux hooks and cart state
- `styled-components`
- `ShoppingCartMenu`, `Button`, `Logo` components
- Theme object with colors

---

### 4. **Footer Component**
**File:** `/src/components/Footer/Footer.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - fully self-contained with hardcoded links

**Children Components Used:**
- `Logo` (with `logoOnly` prop)
- `FooterCard` (for link sections)

**Key Features:**
- 4-column grid layout (responsive: 1 → 3 → 4 columns)
- Navigation links (Home, Categories, About, Login)
- Social media links
- App store badges (images)
- White text on footer background

**Dependencies:**
- `styled-components`
- `Logo`, `FooterCard` components
- Static image imports (googleplay-banner, appstore-banner)
- Theme object with colors

---

### 5. **Badge Component**
**File:** `/src/components/Badge/Badge.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type BadgeProps = {
  text: string
  className?: string
}
```

**Children Components Used:**
- `Body` component (for text display)

**Key Features:**
- Small inline badge with background
- Text is capitalized and styled
- Minimal padding
- No interactive behavior

**Dependencies:**
- `styled-components`
- `Body` component
- Theme object with colors, borderRadius

---

### 6. **Modal Component**
**File:** `/src/components/Modal/Modal.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type ModalProps = {
  isOpen: boolean
  onClose: () => void
  children?: React.ReactNode
}
```

**Key Features:**
- Uses `react-transition-group` for animations
- Portal rendering to #modal element
- Escape key closes modal (via `useKey` hook)
- Body scroll lock when open (via `useLockBodyScroll` hook)
- Close button in top-right corner
- Backdrop click closes modal
- Different animations for mobile (slide-up) vs desktop (scale)

**Dependencies:**
- `react-transition-group` (CSSTransition)
- `Portal` component
- Custom hooks: `useKey`, `useLockBodyScroll`
- `Button` component (for close button)
- Styled components from Modal.styles.tsx

**Modal.styles.tsx Details:**
- `ModalContent`: Fixed positioning with animations
- `Backdrop`: Full-screen semi-transparent overlay
- `TopBar`: Close button container
- Different animations for mobile/desktop

---

### 7. **ShoppingCartMenu Component**
**File:** `/src/components/ShoppingCartMenu/ShoppingCartMenu.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type ShoppingCartMenuProps = {
  isOpen: boolean
  totalPrice: number
  onClose: () => void
  cartItems: CartItem[]
  onGoToCheckoutClick?: () => void
  onItemChange: (item: CartItem) => void
}
```

**Children Components Used:**
- `Sidebar` (container)
- Internal `ShoppingCartMenuItem` component
- Internal `Footer` component
- `Body` component (for text)
- `Select` component (for quantity)

**Key Features:**
- Wraps in `Sidebar` component
- Displays cart items with quantity selector (0-10)
- Shows total price
- Checkout button (disabled if total is 0)
- Item change handler for quantity updates

**Dependencies:**
- `styled-components`
- `Sidebar`, `Button`, `Select`, `Body` components
- `CartItem` type from app-state
- `toCurrency` helper

---

### 8. **TopBanner Component**
**File:** `/src/components/TopBanner/TopBanner.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
```typescript
type TopBannerProps = {
  title?: string
  photoUrl?: string
  onBackClick?: () => void
}
```

**Key Features:**
- Background image support or solid color fallback
- Optional title display (inverted color if photo present)
- Fixed height (240px)
- Centered content
- Responsive background sizing

**Dependencies:**
- `styled-components`
- `Heading` component
- Theme object with colors

---

### 9. **HomePage**
**File:** `/src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - no props

**Children Components Used:**
- `PageTemplate` (wrapper)
- `Banner`
- `RestaurantsSection` (title prop)
- `AwardWinningSection`
- `CategoriesSection` (categories prop)

**Key Features:**
- Landing page layout
- Multiple sections with spacing
- Imports category stub data
- Uses PageTemplate wrapper

---

### 10. **CategoryListPage**
**File:** `/src/pages/CategoryListPage/CategoryListPage.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - no props

**Children Components Used:**
- `PageTemplate`
- `TopBanner` (with title)
- `CategoryList` (with categories)
- `Heading`, `Body` components
- `useNavigate` hook

**Key Features:**
- Uses React Router for navigation
- Back button navigation via `navigate(-1)`
- Stub categories data
- Descriptive text section

---

### 11. **RestaurantDetailPage**
**File:** `/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - uses route params (`useParams<'id'>`)

**Children Components Used:**
- `PageTemplate` (type: "sticky-header")
- `FoodItemModal` (conditional)
- `TopBanner` (with photo)
- `ErrorBlock` (error states)
- `Spinner` (loading state)
- `Heading`, `Body` components
- `Badge`, `Review` components
- `AnimatedIllustration` (for errors)
- `FoodSection` (for menu items: food, dessert, drinks)

**Key Features:**
- Fetches restaurant data via `useFetchRestaurant(id)` hook
- Redux integration: cart items selection and actions
- Three error states: 500, 404, loading
- Modal for food item selection
- Multiple menu sections
- Back button navigation

**Dependencies:**
- `react-router-dom` (useParams, useNavigate)
- Redux hooks and cart actions
- API hook: `useFetchRestaurant`
- Custom components and state management

---

### 12. **CheckoutPage**
**File:** `/src/pages/CheckoutPage/CheckoutPage.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - no props

**Children Components Used:**
- `PageTemplate` (type: "basic")
- `MultiStepForm` (registration form)
- `OrderSummary` (cart items)
- `Heading` component

**Key Features:**
- Two-column layout (form + order summary)
- Uses Redux to get cart items
- Responsive layout (stacked on mobile, side-by-side on desktop)
- Form spans full width on mobile, fixed width on desktop

---

### 13. **SuccessPage**
**File:** `/src/pages/SuccessPage/SuccessPage.tsx`

**Props Interface:**
None - no props

**Children Components Used:**
- `PageTemplate` (type: "basic")
- `TopBanner` (with title)
- `OrderSummary` (with order items)
- `Heading`, `Body` components

**Key Features:**
- Displays order confirmation
- Shows estimated delivery time
- Uses Redux to get order items (`selectOrderItems`)
- Decorative illustration (ladies-sushi.svg)
- Uses order state (not cart state)

---

### 14. **Types**
**File:** `/src/types/index.ts`

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
type ShopItem = {
  id: number
  name: string
  price: number
  imageUrl?: string
  description?: string
}

type FoodMenuItem = {
  id: number
  name: string
  description?: string
  price: number
}

type Restaurant = {
  name: string
  id?: string
  mapsUrl?: string
  rating?: number
  url?: string
  address?: string
  specialty: string
  photoUrl: string
  isClosed?: boolean
  categories?: string[]
  isLoading?: boolean
  isNew?: boolean
  menu: {
    food: FoodMenuItem[]
    dessert: FoodMenuItem[]
    drinks: FoodMenuItem[]
  }
}
```

---

## Key State Management (Cart)

**CartItem Type** (extends ShopItem):
```typescript
interface CartItem extends ShopItem {
  quantity: number
}
```

**Cart State:**
```typescript
type CartState = {
  visible: boolean
  items: CartItem[]
}
```

**Selectors:**
- `selectCartVisibility`: boolean
- `selectCartItems`: CartItem[]
- `selectCartTotal`: number (sum of quantity * price)
- `selectCartItemsCount`: number (total quantity)

**Actions:**
- `toggleVisibilityAction`: toggle cart menu visibility
- `saveItemAction(item)`: add or update item quantity
- `clearItemAction(item)`: remove item entirely
- `removeItemAction(item)`: decrement quantity or remove
- `clearCartAction`: clear all items and hide cart

---

## Common Pattern Summary

1. **Presentational vs Container Pattern**: Header component uses both (HeaderComponent + Header)
2. **Redux Integration**: Header, RestaurantDetailPage, CheckoutPage, SuccessPage use Redux
3. **Styled-components**: All components use styled-components for styling
4. **Theme Integration**: All styled components access theme via `useTheme()` hook
5. **Hooks Usage**: Custom hooks for keyboard (useKey), body scroll lock (useLockBodyScroll)
6. **React Router**: Navigation via Link and useNavigate across pages
7. **Error Handling**: RestaurantDetailPage handles loading, errors, and success states
8. **Modal/Sidebar Pattern**: Uses Portal and CSSTransition for animations

All files are located under `/src/` with components in `/src/components/`, pages in `/src/pages/`, and types in `/src/types/`.

     5→/**
     6→ * The reason we expose our own dispatch and selector
     7→ * is that we get typescript safety anywhere, which is
     8→ * automatically updated as we add more slices to the application.
     9→ */
    10→
    11→export const useAppDispatch = () => useDispatch<AppDispatch>()
    12→export const useAppSelector: TypedUseSelectorHook<RootState> = useSelector

file.content:
import { TypedUseSelectorHook, useDispatch, useSelector } from 'react-redux'

import { AppDispatch, RootState } from './store'

/**
 * The reason we expose our own dispatch and selector
 * is that we get typescript safety anywhere, which is
 * automatically updated as we add more slices to the application.
 */

export const useAppDispatch = () => useDispatch<AppDispatch>()
export const useAppSelector: TypedUseSelectorHook<RootState> = useSelector

import styled from 'styled-components'

import { PageTemplate } from '../../templates/PageTemplate'
import { categories } from '../../stub/categories'

import { RestaurantsSection } from './components/RestaurantsSection'
import { CategoriesSection } from './components/CategoriesSection/CategoriesSection'
import { Banner } from './components/Banner'
import { AwardWinningSection } from './components/AwardWinningSection'

const Spacing = styled.div`
  margin-bottom: 4.5rem;
`

export const HomePage = () => (
  <PageTemplate>
    <Banner />
    <Spacing />
    <RestaurantsSection title="Our favorite picks" />
    <Spacing />
    <AwardWinningSection />
    <Spacing />
    <CategoriesSection categories={categories} />
    <Spacing />
  </PageTemplate>
)

1→import styled from 'styled-components'
     2→import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'
     3→import Carousel, { type ArrowProps, type CarouselProps } from 'react-multi-carousel'
     4→
     5→import { useFetchRestaurants } from '../../../../api/hooks'
     6→import { IconButton } from '../../../../components/IconButton'
     7→import { PageSection } from '../../../../components/PageSection'
     8→import { RestaurantCard, RestaurantCardSkeleton } from '../../../../components/RestaurantCard'
     9→import { Restaurant } from '../../../../types'
    10→import { isMobile } from '../../../../helpers'
    11→
    12→const PreviousButton = styled(IconButton)`
    13→  position: absolute;
    14→  left: 0;
    15→`
    16→
    17→const NextButton = styled(IconButton)`
    18→  position: absolute;
    19→  right: 0;
    20→`
    21→
    22→type RestaurantsSectionProps = {
    23→  title: string
    24→}
    25→
    26→const CustomArrow = (props: Partial<CarouselProps & ArrowProps> & { isNext?: boolean }) => {
    27→  // filter out unnecessary props coming from react-multi-carousel
    28→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
    29→  const { carouselState, rtl, isNext, ...rest } = props
    30→  return isNext ? (
    31→    <NextButton name="arrow-right" {...rest} />
    32→  ) : (
    33→    <PreviousButton name="arrow-left" {...rest} />
    34→  )
    35→}
    36→
    37→export const RestaurantsSection = ({ title }: RestaurantsSectionProps) => {
    38→  const navigate = useNavigate()
    39→
    40→  const { restaurants, status } = useFetchRestaurants()
    41→
    42→  const isOnMobile = isMobile()
    43→  return (
    44→    <PageSection title={title}>
    45→      <Carousel
    46→        draggable={isOnMobile}
    47→        partialVisible={isOnMobile}
    48→        customLeftArrow={<CustomArrow />}
    49→        customRightArrow={<CustomArrow isNext />}
    50→        responsive={{
    51→          desktop: {
    52→            breakpoint: { max: 5000, min: 1024 },
    53→            items: 3,
    54→            slidesToSlide: 3,
    55→          },
    56→          tablet: {
    57→            breakpoint: { max: 1024, min: 464 },
    58→            items: 2,
    59→            paritialVisibilityGutter: 50,
    60→          },
    61→          mobile: {
    62→            breakpoint: { max: 464, min: 0 },
    63→            items: 1,
    64→            paritialVisibilityGutter: 30,
    65→          },
    66→        }}
    67→        containerClass="carousel-container"
    68→        removeArrowOnDeviceType={['tablet', 'mobile']}
    69→        itemClass="carousel-item"
    70→      >
    71→        {status === 'loading'
    72→          ? Array.from({ length: 3 }).map((_, index) => <RestaurantCardSkeleton key={index} />)
    73→          : restaurants.map((restaurant: Restaurant, index: number) => (
    74→              <RestaurantCard
    75→                key={restaurant.name + index}
    76→                {...restaurant}
    77→                onClick={() => navigate(`/restaurants/${restaurant.id}`)}
    78→              />
    79→            ))}
    80→      </Carousel>
    81→    </PageSection>
    82→  )
    83→}
    84→

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import { CSSTransition } from 'react-transition-group'
     3→
     4→import { useKey, useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks'
     5→import { Button } from '../Button'
     6→import { Heading } from '../typography'
     7→
     8→import { SidebarContainer, SidebarContent, SidebarFooter, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Sidebar.styles'
     9→
    10→type SidebarProps = {
    11→  isOpen: boolean
    12→  title: string
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→  footer?: React.ReactNode
    15→}
    16→
    17→export const Sidebar: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<SidebarProps>> = ({
    18→  children,
    19→  footer,
    20→  isOpen,
    21→  title,
    22→  onClose,
    23→}) => {
    24→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    25→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    26→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    27→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    28→
    29→  return (
    30→    <>
    31→      <CSSTransition
    32→        in={isOpen}
    33→        timeout={300}
    34→        classNames="sidebar"
    35→        unmountOnExit
    36→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    37→      >
    38→        <SidebarContainer data-testid="sidebar" ref={contentRef}>
    39→          <TopBar>
    40→            <Heading level={4}>{title}</Heading>
    41→            <Button
    42→              aria-label="close sidebar"
    43→              data-testid="sidebar-close-btn"
    44→              onClick={onClose}
    45→              clear
    46→              round
    47→              icon="cross"
    48→              iconSize={16}
    49→            />
    50→          </TopBar>
    51→          <SidebarContent data-testid="sidebar-content">{children}</SidebarContent>
    52→          {footer && <SidebarFooter data-testid="sidebar-footer">{footer}</SidebarFooter>}
    53→        </SidebarContainer>
    54→      </CSSTransition>
    55→
    56→      <CSSTransition
    57→        in={isOpen}
    58→        timeout={300}
    59→        classNames="backdrop"
    60→        unmountOnExit
    61→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    62→      >

     9→    margin: 0 auto;
    10→    padding: ${spacing.l} 0;
    11→    display: flex;
    12→    align-items: center;
    13→    flex-direction: column;
    14→  `
    15→)

    18→  ({ theme: { spacing } }) => css`
    19→    text-align: center;
    20→    margin-top: ${spacing.m};
    21→    display: flex;
    22→    justify-content: center;
    23→    width: 100%;
    24→  `
    25→)
    26→
    27→type ErrorBlockProps = {
    28→  title: string
    29→  image: React.ReactNode
    30→  body: string
    31→  buttonText: string
    32→  onButtonClick: () => void
    33→}
    34→
    35→export const ErrorBlock = ({ title, image, body, buttonText, onButtonClick }: ErrorBlockProps) => (
    36→  <ErrorContainer>
    37→    <Heading level={2}>{title}</Heading>
    38→    <ImageContainer>{image}</ImageContainer>

    40→    <Button onClick={onButtonClick}>{buttonText}</Button>

src/components/typography/Body.tsx
src/components/typography/Heading.tsx
src/components/typography/index.tsx

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import styled, { css } from 'styled-components'
     3→
     4→type FontSize = 'body' | 'bodyS' | 'bodyXS' | 'bodyXXS'
     5→type FontWeight = 'regular' | 'medium' | 'bold' | 'black'
     6→
     7→type StyledBodyProps = {
     8→  $size: string
     9→  $fontWeight: string
    10→}
    11→
    12→const BodyBase = styled.p<StyledBodyProps>(
    13→  ({ $size, $fontWeight, color: textColor, theme: { typography, color } }) => css`
    14→    display: block;
    15→    font-family: 'Hind';
    16→    color: ${textColor || color.primaryText};
    17→    font-weight: ${typography.fontWeight[$fontWeight as FontWeight]};
    18→    font-size: ${typography.fontSize[`body${$size}` as FontSize]};
    19→  `
    20→)
    21→
    22→type AsElement = 'span' | 'p' | 'label' | 'figcaption'
    23→
    24→type ElementProps<T extends AsElement> = T extends 'label'
    25→  ? React.LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>
    26→  : T extends 'span'
    27→    ? React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>
    28→    : T extends 'p'
    29→      ? React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLParagraphElement>
    30→      : React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>
    31→
    32→type DefaultProps = {
    33→  className?: string
    34→  size?: 'S' | 'XS' | 'XXS'
    35→  fontWeight?: 'regular' | 'medium' | 'bold' | 'black'
    36→  type?: AsElement
    37→  color?: string
    38→  children: React.ReactNode | string
    39→}
    40→
    41→type BodyProps = DefaultProps & ElementProps<AsElement>
    42→
    43→export const Body: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<BodyProps>> = ({
    44→  size = '',
    45→  fontWeight = 'regular',
    46→  type = 'p',
    47→  color,
    48→  children,
    49→  className,
    50→  ...props
    51→}) => (
    52→  <BodyBase
    53→    as={type}
    54→    $size={size}
    55→    $fontWeight={fontWeight}
    56→    color={color}
    57→    className={className}
    58→    {...props}
    59→  >
    60→    {children}
    61→  </BodyBase>
    62→)
    63→

Now I have everything I need. Let me write all the story files. I'll target these 10 components:

1. **Button** - low-level reusable component
2. **Badge** - low-level reusable component
3. **RestaurantCard** - mid-level component with various states
4. **TopBanner** - visual component
5. **Header** - provider-backed component (presentational version)
6. **Footer** - layout component
7. **ErrorBlock** - feedback component
8. **HomePage** - page component with data fetching
9. **CategoryDetailPage** - page component with data fetching
10. **RestaurantDetailPage** - page component with data fetching + Redux

content.text:
For the page stories, the problem is double MemoryRouter — preview already wraps everything in MemoryRouter, and the story decorators add another one. The fix is to not use the preview's MemoryRouter but instead use a custom one. But since the preview wraps all stories, I need to handle the page stories differently. The simplest approach: remove MemoryRouter from preview and let only stories that need routing provide it, OR use a `react-router-dom` approach that's safe to nest.

Actually, the real issue is that the page stories have their own MemoryRouter decorators which nest inside the preview's MemoryRouter. The `useRef` error likely comes from duplicate React instances in the router. Let me remove MemoryRouter from the preview and provide it in story decorators only where needed.

Wait - but then simple component stories that use `<Link>` (like Footer) will break without a router. Let me think about this differently. The issue is specifically about nesting MemoryRouter. Let me check if that's truly the issue by looking at the CategoryDetailPage error more carefully.

content.text:
Confirmed — double router issue. The page stories need to NOT nest another MemoryRouter since the preview already provides one. But the page stories need route params. I need to use a different approach — provide route params without wrapping in a new Router.

The fix: Remove MemoryRouter decorators from page stories and instead use `initialEntries` in the preview's MemoryRouter via parameters, OR use the `reactRouter` parameter to configure routes. Actually, the simplest fix is to just change the preview to not include MemoryRouter and instead have each story file provide its own routing when needed. But that means simple components that use `Link` would break.

Better approach: Keep MemoryRouter in preview but use React Router's `useNavigate`/`useParams` mocking or use `Routes`/`Route` inside the existing router context (no new Router wrapper).

All done. Here's a summary of what was set up:

## Storybook Setup Summary

### Infrastructure
- **`.storybook/preview.tsx`** — Decorators providing Redux store, styled-components ThemeProvider (light theme), GlobalStyle, and MemoryRouter (conditionally skipped via `routerOverride` parameter for page stories with custom routing)
- **`.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`** — MSW handlers mocking the restaurant API (`GET /restaurants`, `?id=`, `?category=`) with 4 deterministic mock restaurants
- **`.storybook/preview-body.html`** — Portal root (`<div id="modal">`) for modal/portal rendering
- **`.storybook/main.ts`** — Added `staticDirs: ['../public']` for MSW service worker
- **`msw-storybook-addon`** — Installed as dev dependency

### Story Files (10 files, 28 stories)
| File | Stories | Type |
|------|---------|------|
| `Button.stories.tsx` | Default, Clear, Disabled, WithIcon, **CssCheck** | Low-level component |
| `Badge.stories.tsx` | Default, LongText, MultipleCategories | Low-level component |
| `RestaurantCard.stories.tsx` | Default, NewRestaurant, Closed, Loading | Mid-level component |
| `TopBanner.stories.tsx` | WithTitle, WithPhoto, NoTitle | Visual component |
| `Header.stories.tsx` | Default, LogoOnly, WithCartItems | Provider-backed (presentational) |
| `Footer.stories.tsx` | Default | Layout component |
| `ErrorBlock.stories.tsx` | Default, NotFound, ClickRetry | Feedback component |
| `HomePage.stories.tsx` | Default | Page with data fetching |
| `CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx` | Default, Asian | Page with routing + data fetching |
| `RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx` | Default, NotFound | Page with routing + Redux + data fetching |

- All stories have `tags: ['ai-generated']`
- All stories have `play` functions with meaningful assertions
- **CssCheck** story verifies `font-family: Hind` on Button
- All 35 tests pass, TypeScript check passes cleanly
